continue_screen.title: Continue this story

l1.banner: Advanced Options — only change if you understand the impact.
l1.temperature_hint: Higher = more creative but less stable.
l1.self_review_desc: Layer 1 self-evaluates and rewrites weak chapters.
l1.self_review_threshold_hint: Chapters scoring below the threshold will be rewritten.

l2.banner: Advanced Options — only change if you understand the impact.
l2.layer2_model_hint: Layer 2 is used to boost drama and analyze story pacing.

features.title: Pipeline Features
features.desc: Enable or disable pipeline features. Changes are saved immediately.
features.overlay_hint: Show a real-time progress banner during story generation.
features.illust_hint: Automatically create an illustration for each chapter when the image provider is enabled.
features.sim_hint: Save transcripts from the simulation stage for display/debate later.
features.drama_hint: Allow drama level to reach 'climax' (higher than 'high'). Increases drama intensity.

flowkit.title: FlowKit (Google Labs)
flowkit.desc: Generate Imagen 3 / Veo images via a local Google Labs session. Requires Chrome Extension installed and Flow login.
flowkit.warning_body: Google Labs may restrict or ban your Google account if automated traffic is detected. We recommend using a secondary account. Check the box below to acknowledge you understand the risk before enabling the provider.
flowkit.ack_label: I understand the risk of a Google account ban when using FlowKit and wish to proceed.
flowkit.enable_desc: Connects via WebSocket to the Chrome Extension on the configured port.
flowkit.refiner_hint: Imagen final refine pass — consumes double tokens

usage.title: Usage
usage.subtitle: Token and cost of the current server session

usage.demo_banner: Real metrics are not available (endpoint requires permission or network error). Displaying sample data.
usage.chart_empty: No models have been called yet.

providers.delete_provider_desc: This will delete profile "{name}" from the list. Cannot be undone.

account.title: Account
account.subtitle: StoryForge usage overview

account.guide_desc: Quick tips for creating high-quality stories.

guide.title: Guide
guide.subtitle: Setup API keys, generate your first story, and frequently asked questions
guide.settings_desc: Configure API keys, models, and pipeline flags.

steps.step_1_desc: Add OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI, or Z.AI. Keys are stored server-side and never appear in URLs.
steps.step_2_desc: Select genre + style, write a brief idea, set chapter count, and click Run.
steps.step_3_desc: Allow readers to make choices in the plot via an interactive branching graph.
steps.step_4_desc: Export stories to formats compatible with Kindle, iPad, or for printing.

faq_items.start_a: Go to Settings first and add at least one API key (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google AI). Then go back to the home page, fill in your idea, and click Run pipeline. Your first story usually takes 1-3 minutes depending on length.
faq_items.keys_a: Keys are stored on the FastAPI backend (in the data/config.json file) and never appear in URLs or localStorage. The GET /api/config endpoint only returns a masked version. You can delete keys at any time on the Settings page.
faq_items.first_story_q: How to create the first story?
faq_items.first_story_a_2: Select a genre (e.g. Xianxia, Modern, Adventure) and style.
faq_items.first_story_a_3: Write a story idea (2-5 sentences is enough).
faq_items.first_story_a_4: Set the number of chapters (5-15 for the first time) and main characters.
faq_items.first_story_a_5: Click Run. Monitor the pipeline via the progress bar on the right. When finished, the story appears in the Library.
faq_items.branching_a: When you open a story in the <libraryLink>Library</libraryLink>, you can enable Branching mode to allow making choices at the end of each chapter. Each choice creates a new branch, displayed as a graph. You can return to parent nodes, undo/redo, and set bookmarks.
faq_items.exports_q: What formats can I export stories to?
faq_items.exports_a: StoryForge supports exporting to PDF (via fpdf2) and EPUB (via ebooklib). In the Library, select a story and click Publish. The file will download in a few seconds.
faq_items.providers_q: Which LLM model should I choose?
faq_items.providers_a: The default recommendation is gpt-4o-mini or claude-haiku for Layer 1 (story generation), combined with gpt-4o or claude-sonnet for Layer 2 (drama boosting). Z.AI is a free limited quota option — suitable for testing. Open Providers to configure the fallback chain.
faq_items.cost_a: Open <usageLink>Usage</usageLink> to see accumulated tokens + USD for the current server session. Data resets when the backend restarts.
faq_items.troubleshooting_q: What if the pipeline is stuck or errors occur?
faq_items.troubleshooting_a_1: Check <providersLink>Providers</providersLink> — if the main model is rate-limited, the system will automatically fall back after a few seconds.
faq_items.troubleshooting_a_2: Open the Console tab in DevTools to view detailed SSE logs.
faq_items.troubleshooting_a_3: If the error repeats, click Retry on the error page or reload the browser — TanStack Query cache will automatically re-fetch.
faq_items.troubleshooting_a_4: Report issues at <githubLink>GitHub Issues</githubLink> with logs from DevTools.

links.api_desc: Swagger UI for all backend endpoints.
links.claude_desc: Detailed explanation of the 2-layer pipeline architecture.

analytics.subtitle: Select a story from the library to view chapter metrics, quality, and event timeline.
analytics.select_story_desc: Analysis runs on checkpoint/chapter data for each story, so you need to pick a specific story.
analytics.empty_desc: Create or import a story in the Library first, then return here to view metrics.
analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.chart_summary: {count} {count, plural, =1 {chapter} other {chapters}}. Total {words} words. {avg, select, 0 {} other {Average quality: {avg}.}}
